items.q: What is the difference between an ice age and a glacial period?
items.correct: An ice age is a long era with permanent ice on Earth (we are still in one); within it, glacial periods (cold) alternate with interglacials (warm) every ~100,000 years driven by Milankovitch cycles.
items.misconception: The ice age ended 10,000 years ago.
items.random_wrong: Ice ages happen every winter when it gets cold.
items.why: We are in an interglacial (the Holocene) within the current ice age — Greenland and Antarctica still have ice sheets. Glacials and interglacials are within ice ages, set by orbital forcing.

items.q: What causes global sea level rise?
items.correct: Two main factors: thermal expansion of warming seawater, and the melting of land-based ice (glaciers and ice sheets) adding water to oceans.
items.misconception: Sea level rises mainly because Arctic sea ice is melting.
items.random_wrong: Sea level rises because of underwater volcanoes pushing it up.
items.why: Floating sea ice already displaces its weight in water — its melting does not raise sea level. The contributors are LAND ice (Greenland, Antarctica) and thermal expansion.

items.q: Why are there no volcanoes at conservative plate boundaries?
items.correct: Conservative boundaries involve plates sliding past each other horizontally — no crust is created or destroyed, so there is no subduction to generate magma.
items.misconception: There are no volcanoes because the boundaries are too cold.
items.random_wrong: There are volcanoes at conservative boundaries — that's where most of them form.
items.why: Volcanism requires magma generation, which needs either subduction or mantle upwelling. Conservative boundaries (like the San Andreas) lack both, but still produce major earthquakes.
Correct answer: Glacier retreat. “Glacier advance” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Greenland Ice Sheet annual mass loss is closest to:
items.why: Correct answer: 280 Gt/yr. “28 Gt/yr” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Global glacier loss 1961-2016 in trillion tonnes:
items.why: The real figure is 9. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
